value.found_quote: At a high level, the common sandbox modes are: - `read-only`: Codex can inspect files, but it can't edit files or run commands without approval. - `workspace-write`: Codex can read files, edit within the workspace, and run routine local commands inside that boundary. ... - `danger-full-access`: Codex runs without sandbox restrictions. This removes the filesystem and network boundaries
value.rationale: The page explicitly describes Codex's three sandbox modes (read-only, workspace-write, danger-full-access) and states they define filesystem and network boundaries for model-generated commands, including spawned commands. This directly substantiates the claim.
